mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising or stays elevated, the discount rate on equities remains restrictive and compresses near-term valuation multiples, so S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative while the tightening impulse persists.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of months with FEDFUNDS above its 6-month moving average, or if the rolling 2-year sign accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent floor signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which reduces equity earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the median forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional median by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand cooling in consumption and housing, and that joint deterioration should coincide with negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal does not outperform either proxy alone out of sample, or if 20-day returns are not negative when both series fall month over month.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while PAYEMS is steady, firms are increasing output without large labor-cost pressure, which should support margins and lift S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises faster than PAYEMS over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if margin-supportive episodes defined by faster INDPRO than PAYEMS fail to deliver positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the signal flips sign after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A strong acceleration in GDPC1 implies real activity is expanding faster than expected, improving earnings breadth and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rises above its prior 4-quarter average
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with GDPC1 growth above its prior 4-quarter average do not precede positive 60-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the median return is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A positive gap between CPIAUCSL inflation and FEDFUNDS implies real policy rates are too low or inflation is accelerating, which tightens financial conditions later and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if high inflation-minus-policy gaps are not followed by negative 60-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal shows no incremental power versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS reflects slowing payroll growth and weaker cyclical demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as analysts revise earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day returns are not negative after month-over-month PAYEMS declines in at least 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears after a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while RSAFS stays weak, housing and consumption both signal demand contraction, and this combined weakness should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS are both below their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in housing and retail does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered cases, or if either proxy alone explains the signal as well as the joint rule.
